title: Malaysian Fish Satay
description: A mildly sweet and savory Malaysian dish featuring coconut milk and sashimi-cut fish, perfect for a midnight craving. This pescatarian-friendly recipe is cooked to perfection on a tava-griddle.
instructions: Prepare the tava-griddle by heating it over medium heat and brushing it with peanut oil.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, lime juice, grated ginger, garlic paste, salt, and brown sugar.
Add the fish strips to the bowl and marinate for at least 10 minutes.
Thread the marinated fish strips onto skewers, if desired.
Place the skewers on the preheated tava-griddle and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until the fish is cooked through.
Brush the fish with additional peanut oil and coconut milk during cooking, if needed.
Serve the satay hot, garnished with sliced limes and cilantro, if desired.
Offer a side of peanut sauce or sambal for added flavor.
